original_hypothesis.title: Permanent Tokenomics Freeze via Prolonged Checkpoint Delay
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: If `Tokenomics.checkpoint()` is not called for a duration exceeding `MAX_EPOCH_LENGTH` (~364 days), it will permanently return `false`. Because `checkpoint()` is required to advance the epoch, the tokenomics system becomes permanently frozen, halting all reward distributions, bond programs, and inflation minting.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While highly unlikely under normal operations, this represents a strict state transition failure mode. A prolonged network outage, governance failure, or extreme gas griefing could trigger this, resulting in a completely bricked protocol.
preconditions: No user or bot calls `checkpoint()` for `MAX_EPOCH_LENGTH` seconds.

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Hard upper bound on time between state transitions with no recovery mechanism.

operational_questions: Is there any admin override to force an epoch transition if this limit is breached?
Can the proxy implementation be upgraded to remove this check if it happens?
validation_requirements: Verify that `checkpoint` permanently fails after the time limit.

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Protocol experiences extreme inactivity or attacker continuously griefs `checkpoint` transactions.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Time since last epoch end exceeds `MAX_EPOCH_LENGTH`.
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Extremely high (requires preventing all users from calling a public function for a year).

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Complete and permanent halt of the tokenomics system.
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High - requires upgrading the Tokenomics implementation.

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
